Sports.detail: Inter Miami has announced that Lionel Messi is experiencing fatigue in his left hamstring, but he is expected to be available for the World Cup.
Sports.headline: Inter Miami says Lionel Messi has 'an overload' associated with fatigue in left hamstring
Cowser hits 2nd game-winning HR in 2 days, giving the Orioles a 9-7 win over the Rays in 13 innings
Sports.detail: Colton Cowser hit his second game-winning home run in two days, securing a 9-7 victory for the Orioles against the Rays in a 13-inning game.
Leylah Fernandez of Canada was defeated in straight sets by Alycia Parks in the first round of the French Open.
Sports.headline: Canada's Fernandez loses in straight sets to Parks in 1st round of French Open

Sports.detail: England easily defeated New Zealand to win the series, with Dean leading the charge.
Sports.headline: England saunter past New Zealand to take series after Dean leads demolition

Sports.detail: A judge has found Wander Franco criminally responsible for abuse but spared him punishment in the Dominican case.
Sports.headline: Rays' Wander Franco criminally responsible for abuse, judge finds, but spared punishment in Dominican case

Entertainment.detail: Ahead of the Philippine release of the horror film Backrooms, CreaZion Studios has launched “The Backrooms Breakout Immersive Experience,” a real-world horror escape room inspired by the viral phenomenon, running until June 7 at Shangri-La Plaza mall.
Entertainment.headline: CreaZion launches Backrooms Breakout experience
American Music Awards 2026 Red Carpet: Stars Take Over Sin City in Style
Entertainment.detail: Stars graced the red carpet at the American Music Awards 2026 in Las Vegas.

Entertainment.headline: Padma Awards 2026: Emotional Hema Malini accepts Dharmendra's Padma Vibhushan, watch video
Entertainment.detail: Hema Malini was emotional while accepting Dharmendra's Padma Vibhushan at the Padma Awards 2026 ceremony.
Shinnosuke Abe, the manager of the Yomiuri Giants baseball team, was arrested for allegedly assaulting his daughter. Chief offense head coach Hideki Hashigami will serve as acting manager.
Entertainment.headline: Giants manager Shinnosuke Abe arrested for allegedly assaulting daughter

Entertainment.detail: John Achkar's stand-up special, 'AAM JARRIB (Tryin'),' is scheduled to be released on May 27 and is an MBC Shahid acquisition. According to the comedian, Arab humor merits a worldwide platform.
Entertainment.headline: John Achkar's stand-up special set for release on May 27

Politics.detail: The U.S. military reported conducting strikes in southern Iran targeting missile launch sites and boats involved in mine placement, amidst ongoing negotiations.
Politics.headline: US military says it carried out 'self-defense' strikes in Iran, including on missile launch sites
Trump Says U.S. and Iran Are Getting Close To Deal
Politics.detail: Details are emerging about a potential deal between the U.S. and Iran, with Trump stating that negotiations are progressing well and the opening of the Strait of Hormuz is largely negotiated.

Politics.headline: Europe Is Slowly Getting Ready to Ditch America
Politics.detail: Shaken by Trump's bad bargains, Europe is beginning to consider distancing itself from the United States.
China's President Xi Jinping has lauded the 'unbreakable' bond with Pakistan during a visit by Pakistan's Prime Minister Sharif to Beijing, emphasizing their close relationship.
Politics.headline: China's Xi praises 'unbreakable' Pakistan ties as Sharif visits Beijing

Politics.detail: Israel's opposition leader, Yair Lapid, has criticized the potential agreement between the U.S. and Iran, stating it fails to meet Israel's objectives regarding the war.
Politics.headline: Israeli opposition leader Lapid says Trump's emerging deal with Iran is 'bad for the region'

Health.detail: Researchers are developing and testing mRNA vaccines to combat different strains of Ebola, offering potential for broader protection against the deadly virus.
Health.headline: Experimental mRNA vaccine shows promise against multiple Ebola strains
New NIH research reveals that semaglutide sparks different responses inside appetite-controlling brain cells
Health.detail: Semaglutide, used in weight-loss drugs, affects appetite-controlling brain cells differently in individuals, offering insight into why these drugs don't work the same for everyone; scientists also found a possible way to extend functionality.

Health.headline: More than 500 children have died in an outbreak that the world is virtually ignoring
Health.detail: A staggering measles outbreak in Bangladesh has resulted in over 500 deaths, predominantly children, raising concerns about the global response and the measures being taken to address it.

Health.headline: Managing the Second Victim in Healthcare
Health.detail: After patient safety incidents, clinicians need confidential, empathetic, and nonpunitive support to prevent burnout and improve care quality, rather than being blamed.

Health.headline: Suspected Ebola cases in DRC pass 900 as health workers face attacks and shortages.
Health.detail: The number of suspected Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo has exceeded 900, with health workers facing attacks and shortages that are hindering efforts to control the outbreak.

Technology.detail: Fujitsu's invention for improving the performance of scientific and technical computing has been awarded the prestigious Prime Minister's Prize at the 2026 National Commendation for Invention, enabling high-speed and high-precision scientific computing.
Technology.headline: Fujitsu's groundbreaking computing technology for accelerating scientific computing wins the Prime Minister's Prize in Japan

Technology.detail: Social media platform Bluesky reported that hundreds of accounts were hijacked to spread fake news articles as part of a Russian influence operation.
Technology.headline: Bluesky says Kremlin is hacking its platform to spread propaganda

Technology.detail: Huawei Technologies Co announced a new approach to narrow the gap with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC), potentially achieving a breakthrough in making advanced semiconductors without cutting-edge equipment, aiming to start making 1.4-nanometer chips by 2031 using “LogicFolding” technology.
Technology.headline: Huawei touts chip breakthrough to close TSMC gap
Experts Push For Stronger Online Safety Laws To Protect Children
Technology.detail: Experts are advocating for stronger online safety regulations to protect children, particularly concerning emerging technologies like AI-powered platforms and chatbots, emphasizing the need for enhanced privacy protections and safer platform designs.

Technology.headline: Pope calls for robust regulation of AI in manifesto that ponders the future of humanity
Technology.detail: Pope Leo XIV has called for robust regulation of artificial intelligence and for its developers to work for the common good rather than profit.

Science.detail: New research using an artificial intelligence (AI) system is helping to develop new gallium-based semiconductor materials much faster than traditional methods, acting like a “smart materials discovery engine.” The study generated multiple, completely new gallium-based semiconductor candidates that were not present in existing databases.
Science.headline: AI speeds up discovery of next-gen computer chips and electronic materials

Science.detail: A new study has found that cows can recognize familiar human faces. This adds to the growing body of evidence highlighting the cognitive abilities of these animals.
Science.headline: Cows Can Recognize Familiar Human Faces, New Study Finds
China issues ethical guidelines to regulate human genetic data research
Science.detail: China's Ministry of Science and Technology has issued ethical guidelines for human genetic data research to protect the rights of participants and promote the healthy development of the field. The guidelines address the ethical, legal, and social issues that may arise from the use of human genetic data.

Science.headline: Macron urges Europe to accelerate quantum and chips efforts
Science.detail: French President Emmanuel Macron is calling for EU governments and research institutes to unite to secure European sovereignty over strategic technologies like chips, quantum computing and AI. He urged French research organizations to form a “European research and technology coalition”.

Scientists have finally spotted the 'butterfly' molecule, completing a 20-year search in the quantum realm. This discovery contributes to our understanding of molecular behavior and quantum mechanics.
Science.headline: 'Butterfly' molecule spotted at last, completing a 20-year quantum zoo hunt

Opinion/Editorial.detail: Meta Platforms Inc. began laying off employees globally, with about 8,000 people, or 10 percent of its workforce, set to be laid off as the company continues restructuring and accelerates its artificial intelligence (AI) investments, reassigning 7,000 employees to new AI-focused teams and closing 6,000 job openings.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: More AI job cuts on the horizon

Opinion/Editorial.detail: There is a growing risk of economic overheating in the US as the artificial intelligence boom expands beyond semiconductors and spills into the broader economy, and if AI spending continues apace, accompanied by a rising stock market, there is likely no way to avoid a widening pickup in inflation.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Inflation is a tax on AI's unfettered spending spree
A compromise anti-dynasty bill
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The present composition of the Senate is one compelling reason why Congress must finally pass the long-delayed anti-political dynasty law, as four pairs of siblings sitting as incumbent senators essentially put four families in control of one-third of the 24-member Senate.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Are Korea’s "Three Highs" Growing Pains or Warning Signs Misread?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial discusses concerns over high interest rates, high inflation, and a high exchange rate in Korea, questioning whether they are unavoidable costs of economic success or overlooked warning signs, especially considering the economic conditions felt by ordinary citizens and the widening income gaps.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Lai Ka-ying's space journey is an inspiration for all Hongkongers to dream even bigger
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Hong Kong's first astronaut, Lai Ka-ying, launched into space on the Shenzhou-23 crewed flight to the Tiangong space station, marking a historic milestone for the city and inspiring its residents to dream bigger.

Education.headline: Malala seeks urgent action from ministers on girls' education gap
Education.detail: Malala Yousafzai addressed the Education World Forum (EWF), emphasizing that 122 million girls worldwide are still out of school and urging immediate policy and funding decisions to address this issue. She stressed that each year of delay means a lost year of education for a child.

Education.headline: Portugal: 25,000 teachers take to the streets to demand respect and good working conditions
Education.detail: Thousands of teachers in Portugal protested, calling for better treatment and improved working conditions. The demonstration highlights ongoing concerns about the state of the teaching profession and the need for greater support for educators.

Education.headline: A historic victory for workers—and for education unions worldwide.
Education.detail: Education International reported on a significant win for workers and education unions globally. The specific details of the victory were not elaborated upon in the available snippet.

Education.headline: UK study visa issuance falls 32%
Education.detail: New government figures indicate a significant drop in UK study visa issuance in Q1 2026, down by nearly a third compared to the previous year, reflecting changes in net migration. This decline raises concerns about the future of international students in the UK.

Education International reported on the united efforts of education support personnel and education unions in the pursuit of gender justice. This collaboration aims to promote equality and address gender-related issues within the education sector.
Education.headline: Education support personnel and education unions unite for gender justice.

Zimbabwe.headline: President mourns Masarira
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa has conveyed his condolences following the death of opposition leader Ms Linda Tsungirirai Masarira.
Zimbabwe is entering a new era of economic competition, with President Mnangagwa declaring that Government.
Zimbabwe.headline: GDP race emerges as new frontier
Gweru embarks on major roads rehab
Zimbabwe.detail: Gweru has started a major rehabilitation project to fix its roads.

Zimbabwe.headline: Commission approves ASB's second hotel acquisition in Zim
Zimbabwe.detail: The Competition and Tariff Commission has approved the acquisition of Makasa Sun by Dubai-based ASB Hospitality LLC.
Bulawayo has bid farewell to national hero Tshuma.
Zimbabwe.headline: Byo bids farewell to hero Tshuma

World.detail: The Israeli army intensified strikes in southern Lebanon, as Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he had ordered the military to escalate its offensive in Lebanon in an effort to “crush” Hezbollah. The airstrikes come as the United States and Iran seek to finalise the terms of an agreement to end the Middle East conflict.
World.headline: Israel escalates strikes in Lebanon as Netanyahu vows to 'crush' Hezbollah

World.detail: The U.S. military announced strikes against Iranian positions just hours after President Trump says more countries should normalize ties with Israel in any Iran deal, tempering expectations of peace. The military said the strikes were conducted in southern Iran “to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces” and included missile-launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to set mines.
World.headline: US military strikes Iran as Trump says negotiations move forward for deal to end war

World.detail: China launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 3 astronauts heading to its space station. One astronaut is set to stay in space for a year to explore human adaptability in long-duration spaceflights.
World.headline: China launches Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 1 of 3 astronauts set for yearlong stay
Gunmen attack Congo hospital treating Ebola patients over relatives' bodies
World.detail: A group stormed a hospital treating Ebola patients in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) forcing medical staff and volunteers to flee and evacuate patients as gunfire erupted nearby. The WHO chief says the Ebola epidemic is outpacing response efforts.

Chinese Tech giant Huawei has achieved a breakthrough that will allow it to make cutting-edge chips within five years, touting the news as a significant milestone in Beijing's effort to circumvent US technology restrictions. Huawei claims this breakthrough will allow them to produce 1.4 Nanometre chips by 2031.
World.headline: Huawei reveals chip design breakthrough amid US sanctions

Finance & Economy.detail: Three companies with trillion-dollar-plus valuations are expected to go public in quick succession, triggering mandatory buying from index funds.
Finance & Economy.headline: SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic are all going public
Smaller stores grapple with how to adapt to changes in SNAP
Finance & Economy.detail: Small retailers are facing challenges as they adjust to modifications in the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.

Finance & Economy.headline: West prepares for extreme measures to relieve pressure on Colorado River
Finance & Economy.detail: Western states are preparing to take drastic actions to alleviate the strain on the over-tapped Colorado River.

Finance & Economy.headline: Rise in shoplifting and theft in UK finds nine in 10 retailers in rural areas targeted
Finance & Economy.detail: Shoplifting and theft are on the rise in the UK, with a significant impact on retailers in rural areas.
Tim Hortons is planning to hire 10,000 local employees and reduce its reliance on temporary foreign workers as it expands with 80 new locations across Canada.
Finance & Economy.headline: Tim Hortons commits to hiring 10,000 local employees, scaling back on temporary foreign workers

Lifestyle.headline: Why Randeep Hooda Is 'In Teenage Love' After Welcoming Baby With Lin Laishram
Lifestyle.detail: Randeep Hooda expresses his joy and love after welcoming a baby with Lin Laishram.
An article about enjoying a slow and peaceful weekend in the hills of Uttarakhand.
Lifestyle.headline: The Art Of Doing Less: A Slow Weekend In The Hills Of Uttarakhand
Jwala Gutta On Donating 60 Litres Of Breast Milk In First Postpartum Year
Lifestyle.detail: Jwala Gutta discusses her experience donating 60 litres of breast milk during her first postpartum year.

Lifestyle.headline: Thailand's Rs 25,000 Visa Lets Indians Stay For Up To 5 Years. How It Works.
Lifestyle.detail: Details on Thailand's new visa program allowing Indians to stay for up to 5 years for approximately Rs 25,000.
A look inside a town in Kerala where a remarkably high number of residents are twins.
Lifestyle.headline: Inside The 'Twin Town' In Kerala Where 1 In Every 5 People Is A Twin
